AssistantImageEvent.description: Typed public event payload for a canonical assistant image block appended to history.

BlobId.description:
Canonical realm-local blob identifier.

The identifier is content-addressed, but storage and GC semantics remain
realm-scoped.

BlobRef.description: Durable image reference owned by transcript/runtime state.

CapabilityId.description:
Slug-validated capability identifier for skill requirements.

Replaces the legacy `Vec<String>` capability lists with a typed
namespace. Parsed at construction, so callers cannot smuggle invalid
identifiers into descriptors or requirements.

CompactionFailureReason.description:
Typed, serializable cause of a non-fatal compaction failure.

Compaction is best-effort: when it fails the agent continues with the
uncompacted history. This enum is the wire-stable projection of the
in-flight [`crate::agent::compact::CompactionError`] plus the post-summary
commit failures (memory indexing, transcript rewrite) surfaced from the
agent loop. Each variant carries the typed cause; the [`Display`] impl
renders the human-facing message consumers used to read off the old
`error: String` field.

[`Display`]: std::fmt::Display

oneOf.description: The LLM call summarizing the history failed.

error_class.description: Typed classification of the underlying agent error.

message.description: Display projection of the underlying agent error.

oneOf.description: The LLM returned an empty summary, so there was nothing to commit.

The host-supplied compaction curator failed to produce a summary, so
there was nothing to commit (there is no LLM fallback).

message.description: Display projection of the curator error.

oneOf.description: Token estimation over the history failed before summarization.

message.description: Display projection of the serialization error.

oneOf.description:
The memory store rejected indexing the discarded history, so the
original (uncompacted) transcript was preserved.

attempted_entries.description: Number of discard entries that were attempted.

message.description: Display projection of the rejection error.

oneOf.description:
Committing the post-summary transcript rewrite failed, so the original
history was preserved.

message.description: Display projection of the commit error.

CompactionRewriteRange.description: Opaque range carried by the typed compaction rewrite semantic.

oneOf.description: Plain text content.
Base64-encoded image content.
Base64-encoded inline bytes used for ingress and live execution.
Durable blob reference used by persisted history/runtime state.
Base64-encoded video content.
Base64-encoded inline bytes used for ingress and live execution.
Provider-readable media URI used for by-reference video input.

Structured JSON content returned by a tool, preserved as JSON rather
than laundered through a stringified `Text` block.

Tools whose result is structured data (lists, records) emit this so the
canonical transcript carries the typed JSON shape. The payload is opaque
pass-through JSON (`Box<RawValue>`, the allow-listed §3 pattern shared
with `ToolUse` args): Meerkat does not impose a closed schema on
arbitrary tool output. The model-facing text projection is *derived*
from this JSON, never the other way around.

data.description: Provider-opaque structured JSON, parsed at most once by consumers.

oneOf.description:
Rendered skill context injected by per-turn skill activation.

The typed `skill_key` preserves the activation provenance in the
durable transcript — activation is never folded into anonymous
operator text. `text` is the rendered skill body; the model-facing
representation is the text projection of this block.

skill_key.description: Canonical identity of the activated skill.

text.description: Rendered skill body delivered to the model.

ContentInput.description:
Input content that can be either a plain text string or multimodal content blocks.

Deserializes from either `"text"` or `[{type: "text", text: "..."}, ...]`.
Provides `From<String>` and `From<&str>` for ergonomic construction.

DeferredCatalogDelta.description: Additive hidden-catalog delta metadata for runtime notices.

ExternalToolDeltaPhase.description: Canonical lifecycle phase for external-tool boundary deltas.

HookFailureReason.description:
Typed reason a hook execution failed (engine-level fault, not a guardrail
denial).

Mirrors the [`HookReasonCode`] precedent: the variant is the typed owner of
the failure cause; the human-readable string is a [`Display`] derivation,
never a separately-stored field.

[`Display`]: std::fmt::Display

oneOf.description: The hook runtime did not complete within its configured timeout.
The hook runtime executed but failed.

message.description: Display projection of the underlying execution error.

oneOf.description: The hook configuration was rejected.

message.description: Display projection of the configuration error.

oneOf.description:
A `pre_*` background hook attempted a non-observe action (patch or deny),
which is not permitted for observe-only background hooks.

HookId.description: Stable identifier for a configured hook.

HookReasonCode.description: Typed reason codes for guardrail denials.

InteractionFailureReason.description:
Typed reason an interaction-scoped run failed (terminal event for tap
subscribers).

Mirrors [`CompactionFailureReason`]: the variant is the typed owner of the
failure cause; the human-readable string is a [`Display`] derivation,
never a separately-carried field.

[`Display`]: std::fmt::Display

oneOf.description: The interaction was cancelled before completing.

The interaction was abandoned (runtime terminated, dropped, or abandoned
with an error) before completing.

detail.description: Display projection of the abandonment detail.

oneOf.description: The interaction's main run completed but turn finalization failed.

detail.description: Display projection of the finalization error.

LlmRetryFailure.description: Typed recoverable LLM failure carried through retry authority.

message.description:
Human-readable diagnostic. The `kind` and numeric fields are the
authority payload; this string is a display projection.

LlmRetryFailureKind.description: Closed classifier for recoverable LLM failures.

LlmRetryPlan.description: Typed retry delay plan selected for a recoverable LLM failure.

LlmRetrySchedule.description: Recoverable LLM retry lifecycle payload accepted by turn authority.

PeerId.description:
Canonical runtime identity for a peer.

`PeerId` is the routing key: the router and trust store key by `PeerId`,
never by `PeerName`. Two peers may legitimately share a display `PeerName`
(per the Wave-B V5 dogma note), but their `PeerId`s never collide — the
underlying UUID is globally unique.

Constructed freshly (`PeerId::new`) for a peer minted locally, parsed
from a hyphenated UUID (`PeerId::parse`) when we've been given an identity
over the wire, or derived from a 32-byte Ed25519 public key when a transport
still authenticates by raw signing key.

Provider.description:
Supported LLM providers.

`JsonSchema` is derived unconditionally (schemars is a non-optional
meerkat-core dependency): config-owned types such as
[`crate::config::CustomModelConfig`] embed the typed provider directly and
derive their schemas without the `schema` feature.

RunInput.description:
Typed input fact for a run boundary.

A run either starts from caller-provided content or resumes from tool
results already staged at the session's pending-continuation boundary.
The pending-tail case is its own typed variant — run-boundary events and
hooks never fabricate an empty-string prompt to stand in for it.

oneOf.description: The run starts from caller-provided content (text or blocks).

The run resumes a pending continuation whose transcript tail is a
staged tool-results message; there is no caller prompt.

SenderContentTaint.description:
Sender-declared content-taint classification for peer content.

This is the typed vocabulary for the optional taint declaration a sender
stamps onto content-bearing comms envelopes (`Message` / `Request` /
`Response`). `Clean` and `Tainted` are the two DECLARED states.

`None` at the carriers (`MessageKind::*.content_taint`,
`SystemNoticeBlock::Comms.sender_taint`, runtime `PeerInput.sender_taint`)
means "the sender made no declaration" — a REAL third state. Receivers
must never coalesce `None` into `Clean`: an absent declaration carries no
trust information, while `Clean` is an affirmative sender claim.

oneOf.description: The sender affirmatively declares this content clean.

The sender declares this content tainted (e.g. it embeds unvetted
third-party material such as web content or tool output).

ServerToolKind.description:
Typed semantic kind of a provider-executed (server-side) tool.

This is the single typed owner of the server-tool identity. Each provider
adapter parses its native discriminator into this enum once at the streaming
boundary, so downstream consumers never re-classify by matching a
`name: String`. Provider-native sub-event detail (e.g. OpenAI's
`web_search_call` vs `web_search_result`) is preserved in the accompanying
`content` JSON, not in this kind.

`ProviderNative` is the verbatim escape hatch for dynamic provider tool
names (Anthropic `server_tool_use` carries an arbitrary tool name that must
round-trip exactly on replay). It is the only variant carrying a string,
and that string IS the typed fact — not a re-derivable label.

oneOf.description:
Provider-hosted web search (Anthropic `web_search*`, OpenAI
`web_search*`). Sub-event detail lives in the evidence `content`.
Gemini grounding via Google Search (grounding metadata block).

A provider-native server tool whose exact name must round-trip verbatim
(Anthropic `server_tool_use` dynamic name and any unrecognized future
server tool). The `name` is provider-owned and replayed unchanged.

SessionId.description: Unique identifier for a session (UUID v7 for time-ordering)

SkillKey.description:
Canonical runtime identity for a skill.

This is the single identity carried across every surface — the wire parses
directly into this struct, tools receive this struct, the registry stores
this struct. There is no slash-delimited string path form.

oneOf.description: Model finished naturally
Model wants to call tools
Hit max output tokens
Hit a stop sequence
Blocked by content filter
Request was cancelled

StreamTruncationReason.description:
Typed reason a best-effort event stream dropped frames.

The variant is the typed owner of the truncation cause; the human-readable
string is a [`Display`](std::fmt::Display) derivation, never a
separately-carried field. `StreamTruncated` is a UI hint — the terminal
event remains authoritative.

oneOf.description: The per-interaction tap channel was full; streaming frames dropped.
A broadcast event stream lagged and skipped events.

dropped.description: Number of events skipped by the lagging receiver.

oneOf.description:
A live transport failed to deliver queued output-audio packets
(e.g. WebRTC RTP pacing-queue backpressure). K16: delivery
degradation is a typed, session-observable fact — never a
transport-local counter.

dropped.description:
Cumulative count of output-audio packets dropped on this
session's live channel.

SystemNoticePeer.description:
Peer identity carried in a typed comms transcript block.

`id` is the canonical routing identity ([`crate::comms::PeerId`]), serialized
as a hyphenated UUID string on the wire; `display_name` is the presentation
label. Keeping `id` typed lets the projection logic consume the identity
directly instead of re-parsing a `String` back into a `PeerId`.

ToolConfigChangeDomain.description: Optional typed domain for tool-configuration change payloads.

ToolConfigChangeOperation.description: Operation kind for live tool configuration changes.

ToolConfigChangeStatus.description: Structured status data for live tool configuration change notifications.

ToolConfigChangedPayload.description:
Payload for tool configuration change notifications.

The typed `status_info` is the sole owner of the change status; display
text is derived at read time via [`ToolConfigChangedPayload::status_text`].

ToolName.description:
Canonical typed name for an LLM-callable tool.

The model-facing wire shape is still the provider's string tool name, but
internal policy, routing, and registry surfaces carry this handle so raw
strings do not become the tool identity owner.

TranscriptEditRewriteRange.description: Opaque current-format range carried by an ordinary transcript edit.

TranscriptRevisionBody.description: Immutable transcript revision body retained by the session-local graph.

TranscriptRewriteCommit.description: Immutable rewrite commit that advances a session transcript head.

TranscriptRewriteReason.description:
Audit annotation carried with a transcript rewrite commit.

The free-form kind is for review, debugging, and provenance only. It never
classifies a rewrite as compaction; [`TranscriptRewriteSelection`] owns that
semantic through its opaque typed compaction range.

TranscriptRewriteSelection.description: A concrete transcript span selected for same-session rewrite.

oneOf.description:
Pre-semantic-marker range retained for source/API compatibility and
decoding prior durable records. New commits canonicalize this input to
[`TranscriptRewriteSelection::EditMessageRange`] before persistence.
Current typed ordinary-edit semantic.

Replace a full transcript from a core-validated compaction rebuild.

The range payload has no public constructor. New values are minted only
by the validated compaction path; deserialization exists solely for the
durable transcript graph and is revalidated against its retained bodies.

TurnTerminalCauseKind.description: Closed machine-owned classifier for why a turn reached a terminal failure.

AgentEvent.description:
Events emitted during agent execution

These events form the streaming API for consumers.

oneOf.description: Agent run started

input.description:
Typed run input: caller content, or the pending tool-results
continuation variant (no fabricated empty prompt).

oneOf.description: Agent run completed successfully

extraction_required.description:
Whether a separate extraction terminal event is expected after this
completed main run.

structured_output.description:
Structured output from the completed run, when schema extraction
produced a typed value.

oneOf.description: Structured-output extraction succeeded after a completed main run.
Structured-output extraction failed after a completed main run.
Agent run failed

error_report.description:
Typed failure fact (class + reason + display message). This is the
single owner of the failure truth on the run boundary; there is no
separately-carried `error` string or `error_class` mirror — wire
consumers read `error_report.class` / `error_report.message`.

oneOf.description: Hook invocation started.
Hook invocation completed.
Hook invocation failed.

reason.description:
Typed failure cause. The human-readable string is the `Display`
projection of this reason, never a separately-carried field.

oneOf.description: Hook denied an action.
New turn started (calling LLM)
Streaming reasoning/thinking from the model
Reasoning/thinking complete for this block
Streaming text from the model
Text generation complete for this turn
Provider-executed tool content surfaced during a model turn.

kind.description:
Typed provider server-tool kind, carried as a nested tagged object
(`{"kind":"web_search"}`, or `{"kind":"provider_native","name":...}`
for the verbatim escape hatch). Nested (not flattened) so SDK
codegen emits a typed `kind` field instead of an inlined enum.

oneOf.description: Canonical assistant image block appended to transcript history.
Model requested a tool call
Tool result received (injected into conversation)
Turn completed
Starting tool execution
Tool execution completed

content.description:
Canonical typed tool-result content. Display text is derived from
these blocks at the consumer edge, never carried beside them.

oneOf.description: Tool execution timed out
Context compaction started.

estimated_history_tokens.description: Estimated total history tokens before compaction.

input_tokens.description: Input tokens from the last LLM call that triggered compaction.

oneOf.description: Context compaction completed successfully.
Context compaction failed (non-fatal — agent continues with uncompacted history).
Budget warning (approaching limits)

Retrying after a recoverable LLM failure.

The typed schedule is the single owner of the retry facts (failure
kind/provider/diagnostic and plan attempt/delay); display strings are
derived from it, never carried beside it.
Skills resolved for this turn.
A skill reference could not be resolved.

reason.description:
Structured reason for the failure. Display text is the `Display`
projection of this typed reason, never a separately-carried field.

skill_key.description: Canonical structured skill identity/reference, when resolution had one.

oneOf.description: An interaction completed successfully (terminal event for tap subscribers).

structured_output.description:
Structured output from the completed interaction, when schema
extraction produced a typed value.

oneOf.description:
An interaction reached an external callback boundary and is waiting for
tool results before the session can continue.
An interaction failed (terminal event for tap subscribers).

reason.description:
Typed failure cause. The human-readable string is the `Display`
projection of this reason, never a separately-carried field.

oneOf.description:
Some streaming events were dropped due to channel backpressure.
Best-effort marker — the terminal event is authoritative.
Live tool configuration changed for this session.
A background shell job completed (or failed/cancelled/timed out).
A typed same-session transcript rewrite was durably committed.

Inbound peer content was committed into this session's context.

Emitted as a pure projection of the typed transcript carrier
([`crate::types::SystemNoticeBlock::Comms`] with incoming direction)
at the moment the agent commits it — a queued delivery appended to the
transcript at run assembly, or a steer delivery applied as runtime
system context at the model boundary. One event per committed comms
block, so a host taint tracker classifies peer ingestion from typed
facts (canonical peer identity + the sender's signed content-taint
declaration) instead of parsing rendered projection text.

The event stream is best-effort observability (see `event_tap`); the
transcript block remains the durable owner of these facts.

kind.description:
Typed comms notice kind (message, request, response progress or
terminal, lifecycle).

peer.description:
Canonical peer identity of the sender, when classification
resolved one (`None` for carriers that hold no peer fact, e.g.
machine-echoed terminal intents).

sender_taint.description:
The sender's signed content-taint declaration, verbatim from the
envelope. `None` means the sender made no declaration — a real
third state that consumers must never coalesce into `Clean`.

means "the sender made no declaration" — a REAL third state. Receivers
must never coalesce `None` into `Clean`: an absent declaration carries no
trust information, while `Clean` is an affirmative sender claim.

oneOf.description: The sender affirmatively declares this content clean.

The sender declares this content tainted (e.g. it embeds unvetted
third-party material such as web content or tool output).

ServerToolKind.description:
Typed semantic kind of a provider-executed (server-side) tool.

This is the single typed owner of the server-tool identity. Each provider
adapter parses its native discriminator into this enum once at the streaming
boundary, so downstream consumers never re-classify by matching a
`name: String`. Provider-native sub-event detail (e.g. OpenAI's
`web_search_call` vs `web_search_result`) is preserved in the accompanying
`content` JSON, not in this kind.

`ProviderNative` is the verbatim escape hatch for dynamic provider tool
names (Anthropic `server_tool_use` carries an arbitrary tool name that must
round-trip exactly on replay). It is the only variant carrying a string,
and that string IS the typed fact — not a re-derivable label.

oneOf.description:
Provider-hosted web search (Anthropic `web_search*`, OpenAI
`web_search*`). Sub-event detail lives in the evidence `content`.
Gemini grounding via Google Search (grounding metadata block).

A provider-native server tool whose exact name must round-trip verbatim
(Anthropic `server_tool_use` dynamic name and any unrecognized future
server tool). The `name` is provider-owned and replayed unchanged.

SessionId.description: Unique identifier for a session (UUID v7 for time-ordering)

SkillKey.description:
Canonical runtime identity for a skill.

This is the single identity carried across every surface — the wire parses
directly into this struct, tools receive this struct, the registry stores
this struct. There is no slash-delimited string path form.

oneOf.description: Model finished naturally
Model wants to call tools
Hit max output tokens
Hit a stop sequence
Blocked by content filter
Request was cancelled

StreamScopeFrame.description: Scope attribution frame for multi-agent streaming.

oneOf.description: Top-level primary session scope.
Mob member scope for flow dispatch turns.

StreamTruncationReason.description:
Typed reason a best-effort event stream dropped frames.

The variant is the typed owner of the truncation cause; the human-readable
string is a [`Display`](std::fmt::Display) derivation, never a
separately-carried field. `StreamTruncated` is a UI hint — the terminal
event remains authoritative.

oneOf.description: The per-interaction tap channel was full; streaming frames dropped.
A broadcast event stream lagged and skipped events.

dropped.description: Number of events skipped by the lagging receiver.

oneOf.description:
A live transport failed to deliver queued output-audio packets
(e.g. WebRTC RTP pacing-queue backpressure). K16: delivery
degradation is a typed, session-observable fact — never a
transport-local counter.

dropped.description:
Cumulative count of output-audio packets dropped on this
session's live channel.

SystemNoticePeer.description:
Peer identity carried in a typed comms transcript block.

`id` is the canonical routing identity ([`crate::comms::PeerId`]), serialized
as a hyphenated UUID string on the wire; `display_name` is the presentation
label. Keeping `id` typed lets the projection logic consume the identity
directly instead of re-parsing a `String` back into a `PeerId`.

ToolConfigChangeDomain.description: Optional typed domain for tool-configuration change payloads.

ToolConfigChangeOperation.description: Operation kind for live tool configuration changes.

ToolConfigChangeStatus.description: Structured status data for live tool configuration change notifications.

ToolConfigChangedPayload.description:
Payload for tool configuration change notifications.

The typed `status_info` is the sole owner of the change status; display
text is derived at read time via [`ToolConfigChangedPayload::status_text`].

ToolName.description:
Canonical typed name for an LLM-callable tool.

The model-facing wire shape is still the provider's string tool name, but
internal policy, routing, and registry surfaces carry this handle so raw
strings do not become the tool identity owner.

TranscriptEditRewriteRange.description: Opaque current-format range carried by an ordinary transcript edit.

TranscriptRevisionBody.description: Immutable transcript revision body retained by the session-local graph.

TranscriptRewriteCommit.description: Immutable rewrite commit that advances a session transcript head.

TranscriptRewriteReason.description:
Audit annotation carried with a transcript rewrite commit.

The free-form kind is for review, debugging, and provenance only. It never
classifies a rewrite as compaction; [`TranscriptRewriteSelection`] owns that
semantic through its opaque typed compaction range.

TranscriptRewriteSelection.description: A concrete transcript span selected for same-session rewrite.

oneOf.description:
Pre-semantic-marker range retained for source/API compatibility and
decoding prior durable records. New commits canonicalize this input to
[`TranscriptRewriteSelection::EditMessageRange`] before persistence.
Current typed ordinary-edit semantic.

Replace a full transcript from a core-validated compaction rebuild.

The range payload has no public constructor. New values are minted only
by the validated compaction path; deserialization exists solely for the
durable transcript graph and is revalidated against its retained bodies.

TurnTerminalCauseKind.description: Closed machine-owned classifier for why a turn reached a terminal failure.

ScopedAgentEvent.description: Attributed stream event wrapper for multi-agent streaming.
ScopedAgentEvent.title: ScopedAgentEvent

WireEvent.description: Event envelope: session_id, sequence, event (AgentEvent), contract_version
WireEvent.note: AgentEvent schema is emitted above. known_event_types stays as a lightweight canonical inventory for surface drift checks.
